name: AdS/CFT correspondence (Maldacena)
summary: Conjectured exact equivalence between Type IIB string theory on AdS_5 x S^5 and N=4 SU(N) super-Yang-Mills theory at large N on the boundary. More generally: a duality between quantum gravity in (d+1)-dimensional anti-de Sitter space and a conformal field theory on its d-dimensional boundary. Provides the only known non-perturbative definition of quantum gravity in a class of asymptotically-AdS settings and resolves the black-hole information paradox by construction within that class.

addresses_puzzles.note: Asymptotically-AdS black holes are dual to thermal states in a unitary CFT; information cannot be lost. AdS/CFT thus settles the information question for the AdS class of black holes, even though the explicit reconstruction of the interior remains open and the analogue for cosmological / asymptotically-flat black holes is harder.

AdS/CFT is naturally formulated for negative Lambda; positive Lambda (de Sitter) holographic duality is not established. Listed as a UV completion that constrains the Lambda problem rather than solves it.

Provides a precise non-perturbative definition of quantum gravity in asymptotically-AdS spacetimes via a dual CFT on the boundary (Maldacena 1997). Does not directly address de Sitter or cosmological QG, but establishes that consistent non-perturbative QG exists in this regime.

touches_benchmarks.note: Holographic QCD models reproduce qualitative features of the hadron spectrum (Regge trajectories, glueball mass ratios) at the 10-30% level.
The bulk theory is gravity (string / supergravity); GR emerges in the appropriate semiclassical limit.

new_scales: AdS radius L_AdS related to negative Lambda; string scale
new_symmetries: large-N gauge symmetry on the boundary; bulk SUSY
introduces.note: N (boundary gauge group rank), 't Hooft coupling lambda = g_YM^2 N, bulk geometry choice. The duality fixes much of the would-be free structure.

history: Maldacena (1997), 'The Large N Limit of Superconformal Field Theories and Supergravity', arXiv:hep-th/9711200, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 231 (1998). Witten (1998), 'Anti-de Sitter space and holography', arXiv:hep-th/9802150. Most cited paper in theoretical physics.

references.citation: Maldacena, 'The Large N Limit of Superconformal Field Theories and Supergravity', Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 231 (1998)
Witten, 'Anti-de Sitter space and holography', Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 253 (1998)
